description: Seed list of real high-severity CVEs from NVD for Day 0 enrichment pipeline.

cves.description: XZ Utils backdoor — malicious code injected into liblzma, allowing remote code execution via systemd on affected Linux distributions.
Fortinet FortiOS SSL VPN out-of-bounds write vulnerability allowing remote unauthenticated code execution.
OpenSSH regreSSHion — race condition in signal handler allowing unauthenticated remote code execution as root on glibc-based Linux systems.
HTTP/2 Rapid Reset Attack — allows attackers to cause denial of service by rapidly resetting HTTP/2 streams (CVSS not formally assigned, widely exploited).
PHP CGI argument injection on Windows — allows remote code execution when PHP is running in CGI mode with specific locale settings.
Log4Shell — Apache Log4j2 JNDI injection allowing remote code execution. Affects billions of Java applications.
JetBrains TeamCity authentication bypass allowing unauthenticated remote code execution.
Jenkins arbitrary file read via CLI, enabling remote code execution.
Windows TCP/IP IPv6 remote code execution vulnerability — zero-click RCE exploitable over the network.
Ivanti Connect Secure server-side request forgery (SSRF) allowing unauthenticated access to restricted resources.
